original_prompt: Please show me my unread emails.
assessment_reasoning: The prompt is short and conveys the main goal (retrieve unread emails), so an agent can map it to a single action (get_unread_emails). However it omits several execution details that could matter: which account/mailbox if multiple exist, what level of detail to show (subject only, full body, attachments), any time range or filters, and whether showing should mark messages as read. The ground-truth action (call get_unread_emails with no args) is a reasonable default, but the prompt is not fully specific about side effects or output format. Therefore it’s moderately ambiguous.

variations.L1: I want to check my messages.
variations.L2: Please get my unread emails and show them to me.
variations.L3: Please show me my unread emails, and after you display them mark those messages as read.
variations.L4: List all unread emails and display sender, subject, and timestamp for each. After displaying them, mark those emails as read.
variations.L5: Call the function get_unread_emails() with no arguments to retrieve all currently unread emails (this should also mark them as read). Then present the results to me, listing for each email: sender, subject, timestamp, and a short body snippet. Do not modify any other mailbox data.
